title: Marc Portal — Async-First Solo Consulting Side Practice
problem: Quebec small operators (tradespeople, civic tinkerers, small biz owners) have everyday problems solvable with small software, but their realistic options are bad: agencies are expensive and meeting-heavy, freelance marketplaces are race-to-bottom with no judgment, and DIY requires time and skill they don't have. On the other side, a senior dev with a 37.5h day job and a family cannot run a normal consulting practice — phone calls, scheduling dances, and relationship-maintenance labor consume the side-gig hours that are supposed to belong to family. The Portal is the architecture that makes both ends work async: structured intake replaces discovery calls, human judgment gates AI execution, live demos throughout replace milestone meetings, and public per-project showcase pages compound as long-tail SEO. Bedrock: family-first, for both parties. Time horizon: ~3 years before AI self-serve likely obsoletes the wedge. Single load-bearing rule: 1 active build + 1 in triage, public capacity counter, no exception path.
analysis: The Portal is a side-practice consulting system designed around an asymmetric architecture: human-as-judgment-layer at intake and crucial decisions, machine-in-the-middle for everything else (status, threaded messages, structured artifact storage, capacity gating), AI as invisible infrastructure during execution (never a touchpoint, never the headline). Bedrock value: family-first, for both Marc and the client. Every architectural choice subordinates to that constraint. Acquisition is asymmetric on purpose — heavy on showcase-driven SEO (long-tail Quebec-local case studies that compound while Marc is at his day job), light on cold Facebook posts (seeding only, diminishing returns after first 2-3 in any group). Each engagement = one structured artifact. Each shipped engagement = one published showcase page rendered from that artifact, indexed by Google in client-natural language. Pricing is per-tier public, with the showcase wall doubling as the living definition of each tier. The portal has lightweight free accounts so clients can save/resume intake drafts, retain context between sessions, and watch their live demo evolve continuously. Triage SLA = 72h. Demo session (Sunday Night Dread, voice→draft invoice MVP slice) is the cornerstone artifact — it replaces the discovery call CTA that all other consultants use, and it ships before any cold post. Single load-bearing rule: 1 active build + 1 in triage, public capacity counter, no exception path — every other rule is negotiable, this one is not. NOTE (2026-05-09): Fleet/tenant primitives (resolveTenant, theme tokens, tenant_domains, AdminFleet, AdminAppearance, AdminTeam, AdminBilling) exist as architecture for Marc's own per-engagement white-labelling — this is NOT a marketplace, there are no paying buyers, and buyer-admin / Stripe / operator-console UI work is explicitly out of scope. The marketplace-shaped admin pages stay reachable by direct URL but are hidden from the sidebar. NOTE (2026-05-21): the six marketplace-shaped feature.json documents (buyer-admin, fleet-foundation, operator-console, runtime-theme-editor, template-volunteer-roster, custom-domain-onboarding) plus platform-spine were removed during the /meta code-first overhaul — they documented surfaces that are not part of the portal's real story. Removal of the dead code itself (AdminFleet/Billing/Team/Appearance, VolunteerApp, tenant functions, their routes) is tracked as a separate task.
implementation: Stack: Vite 8 + React 19 + TypeScript (strict), react-router-dom 7 for FR/EN routing. Standalone repo at portal/ — not part of lifeascode or any other project. Boring-tech mandate enforced: pinned majors in package.json, no UI framework, no CSS-in-JS, no state management library. Design tokens (warm-dark-amber palette) copied as literal CSS custom properties from @majeanson/lac-react — values reused, no code import (per no-shared-code constraint). Bilingual via a single i18n.ts dictionary keyed by Lang ('fr' | 'en'); FR primary at /, EN mirror at /en. Static-first homepage and showcase pages; account-gated areas (intake, engagement canvas) deferred to a later dynamic surface. Hosting TBD (Cloudflare Pages or Vercel — must be Canada-friendly per Loi 25; <$50/mo total). The portal composes 7 generic primitives, each a sibling feature.json: structured-intake-form, async-status-canvas, case-study-renderer, triage-queue, tiered-public-pricing, vibe-filter, self-service-redirect. Composition is feat-app-shell (only Marc-specific feature). First content/demo is feat-demo-sunday-night-dread. No third-party APIs in the critical demo path. No real-time features. The 7 primitives are reusable PATTERNS, not shared code — each future project instantiates from scratch.
successCriteria:
- 7 generic primitives + composition + first demo all reach `frozen` status before first paid client
- Demo session (Sunday Night Dread MVP slice) is live and testable before first cold Facebook post
- First 1-2 clients are FREE and pre-selected (Marc reaches out to a trusted tradesperson; not first-inbound)
- 1-active cap rule is held without exception in first 12 months
- 6+ shipped showcases live by month 12, each tagged with tier + price + hours
- Tier 0 self-service redirect generates measurable referral traffic by month 6
- Marc's family does not register the side gig as a friction in normal weeks (informal but observed)
- Day job remains 37.5h/week
- Decision point at month 12: continue, pivot, or sunset within the 3-year window
- Total operational expenses < $200/month (boring-tech mandate enforced)

decisions.decision: Family-first is the architecture, not a values story
decisions.rationale: Every side-practice shape that requires phone calls, real-time scheduling, or relationship-maintenance labor consumes the family-time the side practice exists to protect. The portal's async-first form-driven structure is the only architecture that lets a senior dev help small operators without either party giving up family time. This is the bedrock — every other decision must subordinate to it. From brainstorming Insight #13.
decisions.recommendation: Treat any feature suggestion that demands real-time response, scheduled meetings, or evening/weekend availability beyond async messaging as a reject-by-default, regardless of revenue impact.

decisions.rationale: By late 2027–2028, AI self-serve tools (Cursor, Claude, Lovable equivalents) likely make small-software translation a self-service problem for most small operators. Marc's wedge is durable for ~3 years, not 10. Declaring this up-front unlocks design moves founders building forever cannot make: cheap infra, no over-engineering for hypothetical scale, no long-tail features. From Insight #36.
decisions.recommendation: Reject 'this will pay off in 5 years' framing. Measure ROI against 'good enough by mid-2029.' Use disposable infra; don't build for users you'll never have.

decisions.decision: 1-active build + 1-in-triage cap, public counter, no exception path
decisions.rationale: Single most important rule in the system. Every other rule is negotiable; this one is not. If broken, the bedrock (family-first) fails — 3 concurrent clients × side-gig hours consumes weekends. With a public waitlist, demand becomes credibility (counterintuitive but true). From Insight #39.
decisions.recommendation: Implement as a hard system constraint in feat-triage-queue. The form auto-disables when at cap. Triage replies offer date-based queueing, never 'I'll squeeze you in.' Even great clients wait.

decisions.rationale: Big consulting firms put a human at every touchpoint — that is their relationship-as-service business model. Marc puts a human only at first contact and crucial decisions. Everywhere else: portal as medium, AI as invisible infrastructure. Humans appear when judgment is needed, not when companionship is needed. Resolves the 'less h2h' paradox — Marc isn't avoiding humans, he's amputating relationship-maintenance labor. From Insight #9.
decisions.recommendation: First contact = Marc's own eyes on the raw form (no AI summary between Marc and a new client's words). All status pings, scheduling, 'just checking in' touchpoints are absorbed by the portal UI — Marc never types them.

decisions.decision: AI as invisible infrastructure, not a feature or a brand
decisions.rationale: Counter-positioning against the 2026 'AI-powered consulting' wave. Everyone else shouts 'we use GPT' — Marc shouts 'you have my judgment.' AI is the plumbing, not the pitch. Clients should feel a senior dev is on this even when AI typed 70% of the code. From Insight #11.
decisions.recommendation: Marketing copy never mentions AI vendor names. Showcase pages credit Marc's judgment, not the tooling. Source code published with showcases makes AI use technically auditable but not advertised.

decisions.decision: Async-only with public deadlines — no calls, no meetings
decisions.rationale: Async absorbs the calendar tax of conventional consulting. Public deadlines (e.g., 'first draft in a few days') keep async from drifting into ghosting. Mutual respect of life-outside-work for both parties. From Insight #2 + Phase 1 bedrock.
decisions.recommendation: All client communication threaded through the portal (no email, no phone, no Slack). Triage replies and status updates are the only async messages. Public 72h triage SLA visible on the page.

decisions.rationale: Pick projects whose deliverable can sit unattended for weeks without breaking. Voice→invoice for trades is fine (no urgency); real-time chat, live booking, time-sensitive dashboards are excluded by category. The wedge isn't 'small biz software' — it's 'software where works-fine-in-2-months-unattended is a feature.' Selecting by temporal metabolism, not industry. From Insight #35.
decisions.recommendation: Triage rejection criterion includes any project requiring real-time responsiveness, complex third-party API integrations in the critical path, or maintenance commitments beyond the 'one round of post-ship tweaks' service definition.

knownLimitations: Single operator — no team, no employees, no scaling beyond what 5-15h/week + AI execution multiplier produces
3-year time box — model expected to obsolete around mid-2029 as AI self-serve matures
Quebec-local positioning narrows TAM intentionally — not a bug
Async-only excludes clients who need real-time interaction
Published 'we don't do' list loses pushy or relationship-hungry prospects by design
Project selection by temporal metabolism excludes most real-time / time-sensitive software
Showcase compounding takes 12-24 months to bear meaningful inbound traffic — early months rely on cold posts and pre-selected referrals
No formal customer support — one round of post-ship tweaks per engagement, then nothing
Marc reads every form himself — total intake throughput capped by his available reading time, not by automation
userGuide: This portal is a working space for engagements between Marc and a small operator. Describe a problem you'd like solved (paperwork, tracking, coordination — anything everyday). Marc reads it himself within 72 hours and replies honestly: yes, no, or 'tell me more.' If yes, you watch the work evolve through this same portal — live demo always up, status flags visible, no scheduled meetings. When it ships, your project becomes a public showcase page (with redaction options to protect commercial data). Free account. No calls, no meetings. Marc has a day job and a family; engagements are async and respect both schedules.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming session 2026-05-06 — captures bedrock (family-first), 3-year horizon, 1-active cap, async-only, AI as invisible infra, project selection by temporal metabolism. Synthesized from 40 insights across Five Whys / Persona Journey / Reverse Brainstorming techniques.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Stack locked: Vite 8 + React 19 + TS + react-router-dom 7, standalone in portal/. Design tokens copied (not imported) from @majeanson/lac-react per no-shared-code constraint. Bilingual via single i18n.ts dictionary.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Published the portal itself as the second public showcase (Insight #32 — Two-Showcase Strategy: SND for projectability, Self-Documenting Portal for authenticity / dev-cred). Renders feat-2026-001 in the same shape as a shipped engagement: tier, hours, the same lifecycle UI.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Progress checkpoint: 6 of 7 primitives + composition advanced from draft → active (intake-form, async-status-canvas now built, case-study-renderer, triage-queue, pricing, vibe-filter, app-shell). 1 primitive (self-service-redirect) is built but not yet advanced. SND demo content remains draft until the working voice→invoice MVP ships (target 2026-07-01). Full route map: /, /en, /intake, /tier-0, /demo/sunday-night-dread, /showcase/:slug, /engagement/:slug. Sitemap auto-generated with all showcases.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Code-first /meta overhaul: the portal's feature.json documents were rewritten from the shipped code and co-located next to it (src/.../<Component>.feature.json). The six marketplace-shaped feature documents plus platform-spine were removed. Ten keeper features remain — app-shell, structured-intake-form, async-status-canvas, case-study-renderer (public project gallery), handoff-ritual, self-service-redirect, tiered-public-pricing, triage-queue, vibe-filter, engagement-runtime — and the engagement runtime has shipped, so the once-static primitives now render against real visitor engagements.

statusHistory.reason: Created during brainstorming session — root feature for the side-practice portal. Status will advance to active once the homepage and SND demo are live, frozen once first paid client ships.
Code-first verification — portal feature documents rewritten from the shipped code and co-located; marketplace-shaped features removed. See the 2026-05-21 revision.

Verification sweep alongside the FEATURE-DOCS gap audit. Root analysis still accurate; ton-passage transparency arc (passage / dossier / au-revoir) shipped this session adds three meta-tinted pages without changing the architecture described here. Total documented feature.jsons now 47 (was 10 at the prior verification).
